banner:
Welcome to the Interactive Learning Booth

Choose a hands-on guide below to explore Grafana 13 features at your own pace. Each guide opens in a new tab with step-by-step interactive instructions.

rules.title: Tour of Grafana 13 Features (Part 1)
rules.description: Explore SQL expressions, Graphviz panel visualization, revamped gauge and table visualizations, and the new visualization suggestions — all with live examples.

rules.title: Tour of Grafana 13 Features (Part 2)
rules.description: Try dynamic dashboards, suggested dashboards, dashboard templates, and saved queries hands-on.

rules.title: BI in Grafana: Semantic Layer
rules.description: Interactive demo of how BI dashboards are enhanced by a Semantic Layer, covering cross-filtering, generated SQL, time-range filtering, and automatic JOINs.

rules.title: Fleet Management: Create an OpenTelemetry Configuration
rules.description: Create a remote configuration pipeline for an OpenTelemetry Collector using Fleet Management. Walk through the setup wizard and deploy an Application Observability integration.

rules.title: Import External Alerting Resources into Grafana
rules.description: Use the Import Wizard to bring your existing Alertmanager configuration and Prometheus-compatible alert rules into Grafana Managed Alerts.

rules.title: Dynamic Dashboards: Explore the New Dashboard Experience
rules.description: Create a dynamic dashboard and explore tabs, auto-grid layouts, conditional visibility rules, rows, the content outline, and the Dashboard v2 code editor.

rules.title: Exploring Grafana Knowledge Graph
rules.description: Browse the Entity catalog to discover services and their health, then investigate issues across multiple entities using the RCA workbench with timelines, KPI drawers, topology graphs, and mind maps.

rules.title: Git Sync: Manage Dashboards as Code
rules.description: Explore Grafana Git Sync by inspecting a connected repository, creating a dashboard that automatically syncs to Git, and cleaning up.

rules.title: Using k6 extensions in Grafana Cloud
rules.description: Learn how to run a Grafana Cloud k6 test that imports a supported extension (using `k6/x/faker` as an example), execute it, and confirm the extension output, without building a custom k6 binary.
